req.title: Bulk import employees — variant 2
req.statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path bulk import where valid rows are committed and invalid rows produce per-row errors (EP), (2) non-CSV upload rejected with 415 (NEG), (3) CSV with missing required header columns rejected with 422 (NEG/DT), (4) a single-valid-row boundary file to probe the minimum accepted payload (BVA), and (5) idempotent retry — uploading the same valid CSV twice must not duplicate records (STC). Together these cover the positive, negative, and boundary obligations and trace to all stated error pathways and idempotency semantics.

tests.name: test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_commits_valid_rows_and_reports_errors
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:hr-admin
No prior employees from this CSV exist in the system
actions: Build a CSV with 3 valid rows and 2 invalid rows (missing required field)
POST the CSV to /employees/import as multipart/form-data with Content-Type text/csv
expected: HTTP 207 Multi-Status or 200 with partial-success body
Response body contains errors list with exactly 2 entries
Each error entry references the originating row number
trace: R-WEB-098: error report generated per row
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session  # provides requests.Session

VALID_ROWS = [
    "emp001,Alice,Smith,alice@example.com,Engineering",
    "emp002,Bob,Jones,bob@example.com,HR",
    "emp003,Carol,White,carol@example.com,Finance",
]
INVALID_ROWS = [
    "emp004,,Brown,,",   # missing first_name and email
    "emp005,Dave,,",     # missing last_name and department
]
HEADER = "employee_id,first_name,last_name,email,department"


def test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_commits_valid_rows_and_reports_errors(hr_admin_session):
    csv_content = "\n".join([HEADER] + VALID_ROWS + INVALID_ROWS)
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_content.encode()), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post("/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 3
    assert len(body["errors"]) == 2
    row_refs = {e["row"] for e in body["errors"]}
    assert row_refs == {5, 6}  # 1-indexed: header=1, valid=2-4, invalid=5-6

tests.name: test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:hr-admin
actions: Build a plain-text file with employee data but send with Content-Type application/json
trace: R-WEB-098 error pathway: 415 when file not CSV
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session


def test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415(hr_admin_session):
    fake_json = b'{"employees": []}'
    files = {"file": ("employees.json", io.BytesIO(fake_json), "application/json")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post("/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 415

tests.name: test_bulk_import_csv_missing_required_header_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:hr-admin
actions: Build a CSV whose header omits the required 'email' column
POST the CSV to /employees/import as multipart/form-data
expected: Response body identifies the missing column(s)
trace: R-WEB-098 error pathway: 422 when header missing required columns
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session

BAD_HEADER = "employee_id,first_name,last_name,department"  # 'email' intentionally absent


def test_bulk_import_csv_missing_required_header_returns_422(hr_admin_session):
    csv_content = "\n".join([BAD_HEADER, "emp001,Alice,Smith,Engineering"])
    files = {"file": ("bad_header.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_content.encode()), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post("/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    missing = body.get("missing_columns", body.get("detail", ""))
    assert "email" in str(missing).lower()

tests.name: test_bulk_import_single_valid_row_is_minimum_accepted_payload
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:hr-admin
No employee with id 'bva001' exists in the system
actions: Build a CSV with exactly one data row (minimum non-empty payload)
Verify the single row is imported
trace: BVA lower boundary: exactly 1 data row
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session
from <helper_fn> import delete_employee_if_exists

HEADER = "employee_id,first_name,last_name,email,department"
SINGLE_ROW = "bva001,Eve,Taylor,eve@example.com,Legal"


def test_bulk_import_single_valid_row_is_minimum_accepted_payload(hr_admin_session):
    delete_employee_if_exists(hr_admin_session, "bva001")  # ensure clean state
    csv_content = "\n".join([HEADER, SINGLE_ROW])
    files = {"file": ("single.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_content.encode()), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post("/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 1
    assert body.get("errors", []) == []

tests.name: test_bulk_import_idempotent_on_retry
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:hr-admin
No employees with ids 'idem001','idem002' exist in the system
actions: Build a CSV with 2 valid rows using deterministic employee ids
POST the identical CSV to /employees/import a second time (retry)
Query total count of employees matching those ids
expected: After second upload, exactly 2 employees exist with those ids (no duplicates)
Second response imported_count reflects upsert/skip — system total unchanged
trace: R-WEB-098: action must be idempotent under retry
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session
from <helper_fn> import delete_employee_if_exists, get_employee

HEADER = "employee_id,first_name,last_name,email,department"
ROWS = [
    "idem001,Frank,Brown,frank@example.com,Ops",
    "idem002,Grace,Green,grace@example.com,Ops",
]


def test_bulk_import_idempotent_on_retry(hr_admin_session):
    for eid in ("idem001", "idem002"):
        delete_employee_if_exists(hr_admin_session, eid)

    csv_bytes = "\n".join([HEADER] + ROWS).encode()

    def do_upload():
        return hr_admin_session.post(
            "/employees/import",
            files={"file": ("idempotent.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_bytes), "text/csv")},
        )

    r1 = do_upload()
    assert r1.status_code in (200, 207)
    r2 = do_upload()
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 207)

    for eid in ("idem001", "idem002"):
        emp = get_employee(hr_admin_session, eid)
        assert emp is not None, f"Employee {eid} should exist after idempotent import"
